commands.review_summary:
The branch updates Google Gemini GenerateContent and managed prompt-cache payload construction so cached-content requests move system/tool configuration to cachedContents and omit it from GenerateContent, with regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main can build a Gemini GenerateContent payload that combines cachedContent with request-level systemInstruction/tools/toolConfig, and the managed wrapper can patch cachedContent after leaving tools in the downstream context. I did not run a live failing Google request in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair is needed; maintainers should handle ordinary review, required checks, and merge gating for the current PR head.

target.title: fix(google): omit request config with cached content
target.body:
## Summary
- Fixes #84919.
- Omits per-request `systemInstruction`, `tools`, and `toolConfig` from Gemini GenerateContent requests when `cachedContent` is used.
- Covers both cached-content entry points: explicit `params.cachedContent` and managed Gemini `cacheRetention` prompt caching.

## Root Cause
OpenClaw had two ways to put `cachedContent` on direct Gemini GenerateContent requests:

- explicit `params.cachedContent`, serialized by the Google transport itself
- managed `cacheRetention`, where the embedded-runner Google prompt-cache wrapper creates a Gemini `cachedContents` resource and patches `cachedContent` into the transport payload

The explicit path sent `cachedContent` while still serializing request-level `systemInstruction`, `tools`, and `toolConfig`. The managed path stripped the request system prompt, but it still let request tools serialize before patching `cachedContent` into the payload. Google requires system instructions and tools/tool config to be set on the cached content resource when `cachedContent` is used; sending them again on GenerateContent can return HTTP 400.

## Fix
- Direct Google transport now treats non-empty `options.cachedContent` as a cached-content request and skips request-level `systemInstruction`, `tools`, and `toolConfig`.
- Managed Gemini prompt caching now includes the request tool declarations and tool config in the Gemini `cachedContents.create` request when tools are present.
- The managed cache key now includes a digest of the cached tool/toolConfig payload, so a cached content resource is not reused across different tool surfaces.
- The managed wrapper now removes both `systemPrompt` and `tools` from the downstream GenerateContent context after it has created or reused the matching cached content.

## Why This Is Safe
Uncached Gemini requests are unchanged: they still send system prompts and tools directly on GenerateContent. Cached Gemini requests still include `contents` and generation settings such as temperature, max tokens, and thinking config.

Security and runtime controls are unchanged for auth resolution, guarded model fetch, request URL/header construction, tool execution policy, and Slack/channel policy. This fix changes provider payload construction and managed cache contents only; it does not rely on prompt text to enforce the policy.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Gemini GenerateContent requests that use managed `cacheRetention: "long"` no longer send per-request `systemInstruction`, `tools`, or `toolConfig` after the wrapper creates a Gemini cached-content resource. Google rejects that combination with:

```text
CachedContent can not be used with GenerateContent request setting system_instruction, tools or tool_config.
```

Real environment tested:
- Host: local macOS checkout.
- OpenClaw checkout under test: PR head after managed-cache fix (`198a42bbc6`).
- Code under test: `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/google-prompt-cache.ts` and `extensions/google/transport-stream.ts`.
- Model/provider: `google/gemini-2.5-flash` via the real Google Gemini API.
- Auth source: trusted OpenClaw runtime dotenv resolved a Gemini API key; secret value was not logged.
- Slack transport attempt: two real Slack bot tokens and one app-level token from local `.env`, with both bots installed in the same workspace/channel; IDs redacted below.

Exact live managed-cache steps after this patch:
1. Called `prepareGooglePromptCacheStreamFn` with `extraParams: { cacheRetention: "long" }` for `google/gemini-2.5-flash`.
2. Invoked the returned managed stream wrapper with a large system prompt, one tool declaration, and `toolChoice: "auto"`.
3. Let OpenClaw create the Gemini `cachedContents` resource through the managed prompt-cache path, not by pre-supplying `params.cachedContent`.
4. Captured the managed wrapper's downstream context and final Google transport payload shape.
5. Verified Google accepted the GenerateContent request, returned a model response, and reported `cachedContentTokenCount` mapped to `cacheRead`.
6. Deleted the Gemini `cachedContents` resource after the run.

Redacted live managed-cache log excerpt:

```text
managed cache input:
  extraParams.cacheRetention: long
  wrapperCreated: true
  request context before wrapper: systemPrompt present, tools present, toolChoice=auto

managed cachedContents result:
  entryStatus: ready
  cachedContent prefix: cachedContents
  cacheRetention: long
  cacheConfigDigest present: true
  cacheConfigDigest length: 263

managed wrapper downstream context after cache creation:
  systemPrompt present: false
  tools present: false
  options.cacheRetention: null
  options.toolChoice: auto

OpenClaw GenerateContent payload shape after managed wrapper patch:
  cachedContent: present
  systemInstruction key present: false
  tools key present: false
  toolConfig key present: false
  generationConfig key present: true

GenerateContent result:
  ok: true
  stopReason: stop
  contentTypes: text
  response text marker: OPENCLAW_GEMINI_MANAGED_CACHE_14KU17
  usage.input: 26
  usage.output: 17
  usage.cacheRead: 8569
  usage.cacheWrite: 0
  usage.totalTokens: 8612

cleanup:
  deletedCachedContent: true
  delete status: 200
```

Observed result after fix: the real managed `cacheRetention: "long"` path created a cached-content resource, patched `cachedContent` onto the GenerateContent payload, omitted all three conflicting request-level keys, and received a successful Gemini response with `cacheRead=8569`.

Additional explicit `cachedContent` proof:

```text
create cachedContents request shape:
  model: models/gemini-2.5-flash
  contents: present, large stable cached prefix
  systemInstruction: present at cache creation
  tools: present at cache creation
  toolConfig: present at cache creation
  ttl: 300s

OpenClaw GenerateContent payload shape after patch:
  cachedContent: present
  systemInstruction key present: false
  tools key present: false
  toolConfig key present: false
  generationConfig key present: true

GenerateContent result:
  ok: true
  stopReason: stop
  usage.cacheRead: 6990
  cleanup.deletedCachedContent: true
```

Slack two-bot proof attempt:

```text
SLACK_CHANNEL_ID present: true
Slack app token present: true
slack_token1: auth.ok=true team=<redacted> user=<redacted> bot=<redacted>
slack_token1: channel.ok=true isMember=true isChannel=true
slack_token3: auth.ok=true team=<redacted> user=<redacted> bot=<redacted>
slack_token3: channel.ok=true isMember=true isChannel=true
```

Command shape:

```text
OPENCLAW_QA_SLACK_CHANNEL_ID=<redacted channel id>
OPENCLAW_QA_SLACK_DRIVER_BOT_TOKEN=<redacted xoxb for driver bot>
OPENCLAW_QA_SLACK_SUT_BOT_TOKEN=<redacted xoxb for SUT bot>
OPENCLAW_QA_SLACK_SUT_APP_TOKEN=<redacted xapp token>
OPENCLAW_QA_SLACK_CAPTURE_CONTENT=1
OPENCLAW_QA_REDACT_PUBLIC_METADATA=0
pnpm openclaw qa slack \
  --credential-source env \
  --scenario slack-canary \
  --provider-mode live-frontier \
  --model google/gemini-2.5-flash \
  --alt-model google/gemini-2.5-flash \
  --output-dir .artifacts/slack-gemini-live-proof-20260521T132154Z
```

Slack observed behavior:
- The driver bot posted two real Slack messages into the configured channel asking the SUT bot to reply with unique `SLACK_QA_ECHO_*` markers.
- The lane failed with `timed out after 45000ms waiting for Slack message; retried 1x` and `slack-qa-observed-messages.json` was empty.

The Slack attempt did not produce a successful reply, so it is recorded as a transport-path attempt rather than the passing provider proof. The passing live managed Gemini proof above exercises the reported `cacheRetention` provider path directly.

## Tests
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/google-prompt-cache.test.ts extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts -- -t "google prompt cache|cachedContent|guarded fetch transport"`
- `git diff --check`
- IDE lints for changed files: no diagnostics

## Out Of Scope
- Changing Gemini cache TTL or refresh timing.
- Changing Slack/Socket Mode handling or two-bot QA harness behavior.
- Adding compatibility fallbacks that send dynamic tools or system prompts alongside `cachedContent`.

## Transparency
AI-assisted change; locally reviewed and validated with focused tests plus live managed Gemini provider proof.
